Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object initialized with `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `toString` method on a `Number` object initialized with `NaN` (Not-a-Number). It verifies that calling `toString` on a `Number` object containing `NaN` returns the string "NaN". This test ensures that the `toString` method correctly handles `NaN` values, which is important for maintaining expected behavior in JavaScript applications. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `Number` object's behavior.
